ddls:
CREATE TABLE "materials" (
  "material_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each material */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the material */,
  "density" REAL /* Density of the material in kg/m³ */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Temperature of the material in Kelvin */,
  "viscosity" REAL /* Viscosity of the material in Pa·s */,
  "luminosity" REAL /* Luminosity of the material in lumens */,
  "rarity" TEXT /* Rarity of the material (e.g., RARE, COMMON, UNCOMMON) */,
  "is_gaseous" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the material is gaseous */,
  "block" TEXT /* Block or container in which the material is stored */,
  "chemical_formula" TEXT /* Chemical formula of the material */,
  "melting_point" REAL /* Melting point of the material in Kelvin */,
  "boiling_point" REAL /* Boiling point of the material in Kelvin */,
  "conductivity" REAL /* Electrical conductivity of the material in S/m */,
  "magnetic_permeability" REAL /* Magnetic permeability of the material in H/m */,
  "thermal_conductivity" REAL /* Thermal conductivity of the material in W/(m·K) */,
  "toxicity" TEXT /* Toxicity level of the material (e.g., HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW) */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the material (e.g., natural, synthetic) */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the material was first added to the system */,
  "last_updated_date" TEXT /* Date when the material was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("material_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_materials_rarity FOREIGN KEY ("rarity") REFERENCES rarity_levels ("rarity_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "blocks" (
  "block_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each block */,
  "block_name" TEXT /* Name of the block or container */,
  "material_id" INTEGER /* ID of the material stored in the block */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the block or container */,
  "storage_conditions" TEXT /* Storage conditions required for the material */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the block or container (e.g., lab, warehouse) */,
  "capacity" REAL /* Capacity of the block or container in liters or cubic meters */,
  "current_quantity" REAL /* Current quantity of the material in the block or container */,
  "last_inspected_date" TEXT /* Date when the block or container was last inspected */,
  "inspected_by" TEXT /* User who last inspected the block or container */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the block or container (e.g., active, maintenance) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("block_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_blocks_material_id FOREIGN KEY ("material_id") REFERENCES materials ("material_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "rarity_levels" (
  "rarity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rarity level */,
  "rarity_name" TEXT /* Name of the rarity level (e.g., RARE, COMMON, UNCOMMON) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the rarity level */,
  "availability" TEXT /* Availability of the material (e.g., limited, abundant) */,
  "cost" REAL /* Cost per unit of the material */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rarity_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, engineer, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department to which the user belongs (e.g., R&D, Quality Control, Manufacturing) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the user account was created */,
  "last_active_date" TEXT /* Date when the user last logged in */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "material_id" INTEGER /* ID of the material being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the material */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the material was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "device" TEXT /* Device used for access (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  "session_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the access session in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_material_id FOREIGN KEY ("material_id") REFERENCES materials ("material_id")
)
